Items.Title: 在 React Native 应用程序中利用 AWS Amplify 的推送通知功能 | Take Advantage of AWS Amplify’s Push Notification Feature in a React Native App
Items.Description:
在当今动态的商业环境中，通过各种沟通渠道吸引客户非常重要。 从电子邮件和短信到推送通知和语音消息，与受众保持联系是蓬勃发展的关键。

本指南重点介绍推送通知渠道以及将这些通知发送到移动应用程序的流程。 尽管我们将重点关注使用 React Native 构建的 IOS 应用程序，但 Android 应用程序的概念本质上是相同的。

我们将创建一个迷你 React 本机应用程序，并使用 AWS Amplify 推送通知功能及其易于使用的 API 在我们的应用程序中设置和接收推送通知。 在幕后，Amplify 推送通知使用 AWS Pinpoint，这是一项 AWS 服务，可作为我们对用户进行细分和定位的方法。

我们将首先设置并获取推送令牌。 然后，我们将使用这些令牌从 AWS pinpoint 仪表板发送推送通知。 然后，我们将展示如何使用 AWS 库在迷你 Node.js 应用程序中执行相同的操作。

先决条件

您需要了解以下信息才能继续阅读本指南：

  • 熟悉 React 和 TypeScript
  • 了解使用 React Native 进行基本移动应用程序开发
  • 熟悉 Restful API 和 HTTP

为了能够在本地复制本指南，您需要以下内容：

  • 用于开发的配备 Xcode 13.4+ 的 MacBook 和/或 iPhone。
  • 一个Apple开发者帐户，已注册会员计划。
  • 一个 AWS 账户。

推送通知的工作原理

下图说明了向移动设备发送推送通知的主要步骤。 了解此图可以更轻松地开始和调整应用程序中的内容。 该图将构成本指南中所有决策的基础。 我们将简要解释它在基本层面上是如何工作的，然后基于此解释，我们将了解需要做什么来构建我们的移动应用程序以使此流程成为可能。

让我们一步步简单地解释一下发送推送通知的工作原理：

  1. 首先，操作系统（在我们的例子中为 iOS）询问用户是否想要接收通知。
  2. 如果用户接受，应用程序将与设备操作系统交互。
  3. 接下来，操作系统通过 HTTP 请求“推送令牌”——这只是一个字符串，用于向移动应用程序的设备发送通知。 该令牌从推送服务获取，然后发送回移动应用程序
  4. 应用程序通过互联网（例如使用 HTTP）将此令牌发送到后端 Web 服务器。
  5. 当令牌到达服务器时，根据其配置和偏好，服务器可以存储令牌并将其与某个数据库服务器上的用户数据相关联。 每当服务器希望向用户发送推送通知时，它都会检索推送令牌，编写要发送到设备的消息，并将其传递给推送提供程序（在我们的示例中为 AWS pinpoint）。
  6. 然后，它修改消息的配置，包括发送消息所需的任何其他凭据，然后将其发送到推送服务。
  7. 然后，推送服务将其传送到设备，并显示在用户的设备上。 当通知到达设备时，根据其目标移动应用程序是否打开（应用程序在前台）或未打开（应用程序关闭或在后台），会相应地处理此行为。 在本指南的后面部分，我们将了解如何处理所有这些情况。

您可能想知道为什么后端（我们的自定义 API）不直接将消息请求直接发送到推送服务。 为什么要先经过推送提供商呢？

嗯，从技术上讲，后端可以直接向推送服务发送通知请求。 然而，这样做的话，后端就会错过通知提供者提供的好处。 这些优势包括分析、跟踪用户收到通知时的指标、开展活动（在特定时间向用户发送消息）、管理用户旅程等。 这些功能是企业发送通知时的常见要求。

开发环境

截至撰写本文时，该项目已在运行 macOS 13.3、Xcode 14.7 的 Intel MacBook 和运行 IOS 16.5 的 iPhone 上进行了测试。

项目设置

稍后我们将重新审视推送通知图，但现在让我们创建一个 React 本机项目。

在终端中运行以下命令：

 npx react-native@latest init notificationsapp

上面的命令在名为notificationsapp文件夹中创建一个裸露的 React Native 移动应用程序。 请随意给您的名称起任何适合您的名称。

接下来，让我们预览一下这个应用程序。 请记住，我们只会关注 IOS 部分。 我们可以从配备 M1 或 M2 芯片的 MacBook 上的模拟器获取推送令牌。 您可以直接在模拟器上运行该应用程序。

在终端中运行以下命令：

如果您在配备英特尔处理器的 MacBook 上运行，我们需要在实际设备上预览才能获取推送令牌。 打开新创建的项目中的ios文件夹，并在 Xcode 中打开 .xcworkspace 文件夹，然后按照本指南在您的设备上启动并运行它。

您应该会看到该应用程序在您的设备上运行，如下所示。

现在我们已经启动并运行了应用程序，让我们使用以下内容更新App.tsx文件：

 import React, { useEffect } from "react"; import { SafeAreaView, ScrollView, StatusBar, StyleSheet, Text, useColorScheme, } from "react-native"; import { Colors } from "react-native/Libraries/NewAppScreen"; import { handleBadgeUpdate } from "."; function App(): JSX.Element { const isDarkMode = useColorScheme() === "dark"; const backgroundStyle = { backgroundColor: isDarkMode ? Colors.darker : Colors.lighter, }; return ( <SafeAreaView style={backgroundStyle}> <StatusBar barStyle={isDarkMode ? "light-content" : "dark-content"} backgroundColor={backgroundStyle.backgroundColor} /> <ScrollView contentInsetAdjustmentBehavior="automatic" style={backgroundStyle} > <Text style={[ styles.sectionTitle, { color: isDarkMode ? "white" : "black", }, ]} > ios notifications example 😊 </Text> </ScrollView> </SafeAreaView> ); } const styles = StyleSheet.create({ sectionTitle: { fontSize: 24, fontWeight: "600", marginTop: 70, textAlign: "center", }, }); export default App;

一旦我们的应用程序刷新，它应该如下图所示。

设置放大

您需要在本地配置 Amplify CLI，并将其连接到您的 AWS 账户。 在终端中运行以下命令：

 npm install -g @aws-amplify/cli

接下来，运行amplify configure命令来配置您的 AWS Amplify 环境。 按照提示创建新的 IAM 用户，AWS Amplify 将使用该用户来管理您的应用程序的资源。 以下链接包含有关如何配置 Amplify CLI 的分步详细信息。

现在 CLI 已准备就绪，让我们转到 React Native 项目的根目录并运行以下命令：

该命令将要求您在此处为您的应用程序选择一个名称，我将其命名为notificationsapp 。 完成此操作后，它将在项目的根目录下创建amplify和src文件夹。 我们只对src/aws-exports.js文件的内容感兴趣，稍后将使用该文件来配置 Amplify 库。

您还应该在您的 AWS 账户上看到一个新创建的 amplify 项目，如下所示。

添加放大推送通知

接下来，我们需要将推送通知功能集成到我们的应用程序中。 为了添加此功能，我们需要做一些事情，但首先，让我们再次仔细看看我们的推送通知图。

该图没有指定我们的应用程序如何安装在用户的设备上，因此我们的应用程序需要有一个配置文件。 这就是为什么我们可以在 iOS 设备上构建并运行我们的应用程序。

当您准备好将应用程序推送到应用程序商店时，您需要从您的开发者帐户创建一个应用程序，但由于我们只是进行测试，因此我们可以使用开发期间创建的默认应用程序。

当操作系统向推送服务请求推送令牌时，推送服务不会仅仅响应该请求并随机生成令牌，因为操作系统请求它。 它需要了解应用程序并确保它支持接收推送通知。 它需要一个应用程序或捆绑包 ID 才能执行此操作。

让我们创建一个支持推送通知的应用程序 ID，并将此功能从 Xcode 添加到我们的应用程序中。

创建支持推送通知的应用程序 ID

查看您的Apple 开发者帐户并从页面中选择标识符。

单击加号图标创建一个类型为App的新应用程序 ID。

添加应用程序 ID 的描述和名称。 这里我们称之为com.notiapp.noti12345 。 滚动到页面底部并检查“功能”部分中的推送通知。

查看所有更改，然后单击“注册”按钮来注册您的新应用程序 ID，如下所示。

您现在应该在标识符部分看到一个新创建的应用程序 ID。

现在，转到 Xcode 上的<YOUR-PROJECT-NAME>.xcworkspace文件，然后在“签名和功能”选项卡中，使用新创建的应用程序 ID 更新包标识符。 接下来，单击“+ 功能”按钮。

接下来，通过搜索并选择来添加推送通知功能。

推送通知功能意味着我们还希望我们的应用程序在后台运行时接收通知。 让我们向应用程序添加后台功能，并通过搜索并选择后台模式功能，然后检查远程通知来打开远程通知。

现在就这些了。

生成绑定到我们的应用程序 ID 的 SSL 证书

在推送通知图中，当推送通知提供者向推送服务发送请求以使用推送令牌向设备发送通知时，推送服务不会只是随机响应来自任何随机服务器的消息发送请求，因为它可以恶意尝试发送垃圾邮件或误导用户。 我们需要生成一个与我们的应用程序 ID 绑定的 SSL 证书，并且只有推送提供程序和推送服务知道，以便每次推送提供程序想要发送消息时，它都会提供此证书来验证请求并发送向目标设备发送消息。

让我们创建并下载证书签名请求。 从您的设备实用程序转到您的钥匙串。 创建一个并下载文件，如下所示。

前往您的Apple 开发者帐户的“标识符”部分，然后选择您新创建的应用程序。 在容量选项卡中，滚动到推送通知并单击配置，如下所示。

在“生产 SSL 证书”下，单击“创建证书”按钮。

上传证书签名请求文件以创建新证书，然后下载生成的证书并将其保存到本地计算机。

双击下载的文件将其安装到您的钥匙串上。

打开您的钥匙串应用程序并搜索已安装的证书。 证书的名称将与您的应用程序 ID 相同。 单击“文件”菜单栏中的“导出项目”并以.p12格式导出。 记下该文件的路径指向，我们稍后将使用它。

此步骤仅适用于使用基于 Intel 的 MacBook 进行开发的用户。 由于这些用户需要在实际设备上测试他们的应用程序才能获得推送令牌，因此他们需要在Apple 开发者帐户上注册他们的设备才能接收推送通知，并在开发时出现错误，如下所示。

注册您的设备

转到开发者帐户的“证书、标识符和配置文件”部分，然后从菜单中单击“设备” 。 单击加号图标添加设备，指定设备平台、您选择的随机名称和设备UDID ，然后单击注册，如下所示。

现在我们可以继续向我们的应用程序添加推送通知。 在项目的根目录下，在终端中运行以下命令：

 amplify add notifications

在提示中，选择启用 Apple 推送通知通道，然后选择一个证书并指定带有.p12扩展名的证书的相对路径，如下所示。

一旦成功， amplify文件夹将相应更新，并且我们的新 AWS pinpoint 应用程序将在我们的仪表板上创建，如下所示

接下来，让我们安装完成设置所需的必要库。 在终端中运行以下命令：

 npm install aws-amplify @aws-amplify/rtn-push-notification amazon-cognito-identity-js @react-native-community/netinfo @react-native-async-storage/async-storage react-native-get-random-values react-native-url-polyfill`

让我们更新我们的index.js文件以使用react-native-get-random-values和react-native-url-polyfill模块。

 import 'react-native-get-random-values'; import 'react-native-url-polyfill/auto';

接下来，让我们将所有已安装的模块链接到我们的 ios 目录。 在项目的根目录运行以下命令：

如果你收到这样的错误：

请记住，Amplify 推送通知仅适用于部署目标至少为 iOS 13.0 的 iOS 应用程序。 要解决此问题，请打开应用程序中的ios文件夹并更新 podfile，如下所示：

 require_relative '../node_modules/react-native/scripts/react_native_pods' require_relative '../node_modules/@react-native-community/cli-platform-ios/native_modules' platform :ios, "13.0" prepare_react_native_project!

然后在您的ios文件夹中运行以下命令来清除现有的 pod：

最后，运行这个：

这应该可以解决问题。

现在在 XCode 中打开<YOUR PROJECT NAME>.xcworkspace文件并进行以下更新。

更新Appdeligate.m文件以匹配以下内容：

 #import "AppDelegate.h" #import <React/RCTBundleURLProvider.h> #import "AmplifyPushNotification.h" @implementation AppDelegate - (BOOL)application:(UIApplication *)application didFinishLaunchingWithOptions:(NSDictionary *)launchOptions { self.moduleName = @"notificationsapp"; // You can add your custom initial props in the dictionary below. // They will be passed down to the ViewController used by React Native. self.initialProps = @{}; return [super application:application didFinishLaunchingWithOptions:launchOptions]; } - (NSURL *)sourceURLForBridge:(RCTBridge *)bridge { #if DEBUG return [[RCTBundleURLProvider sharedSettings] jsBundleURLForBundleRoot:@"index"]; #else return [[NSBundle mainBundle] URLForResource:@"main" withExtension:@"jsbundle"]; #endif } /// This method controls whether the `concurrentRoot`feature of React18 is turned on or off. /// /// @see: https://reactjs.org/blog/2022/03/29/react-v18.html /// @note: This requires to be rendering on Fabric (ie on the New Architecture). /// @return: `true` if the `concurrentRoot` feature is enabled. Otherwise, it returns `false`. - (BOOL)concurrentRootEnabled { return true; } - (void)application:(UIApplication *)application didRegisterForRemoteNotificationsWithDeviceToken:(NSData *)deviceToken { [AmplifyPushNotification didRegisterForRemoteNotificationsWithDeviceToken:deviceToken]; } - (void)application:(UIApplication *)application didReceiveRemoteNotification:(NSDictionary *)userInfo fetchCompletionHandler:(void (^)(UIBackgroundFetchResult result))completionHandler { [AmplifyPushNotification didReceiveRemoteNotification:userInfo withCompletionHandler:completionHandler]; } @end

该文件是任何 iOS 项目的根文件。 它保存我们的应用程序在启动期间使用的代码和配置以及我们的应用程序和操作系统之间的交互，无论我们的应用程序是否正在运行。

我们使用 Amplify 通知模型中的一些导入更新了它。 我们添加了两种方法来处理推送令牌的注册和处理传入通知。

构建前端

到目前为止，我们更多地关注事物的配置部分。 我们已经知道推送通知的工作原理，因此我们将对index.js和App.tsx文件进行更改，然后将这些更改映射到推送通知图中的步骤。

更新您的index.js文件以匹配以下内容：

 import 'react-native-get-random-values'; import 'react-native-url-polyfill/auto'; import {Alert, AppRegistry} from 'react-native'; import App from './App'; import {name as appName} from './app.json'; import {Amplify, Notifications} from 'aws-amplify'; import awsconfig from './src/aws-exports'; Amplify.configure(awsconfig); Notifications.Push.enable(); AppRegistry.registerComponent(appName, () => App);

接下来，更新您的App.tsx文件以匹配以下内容：

 import { Notifications } from "aws-amplify"; import React, { useEffect } from "react"; import { Alert, SafeAreaView, ScrollView, StatusBar, StyleSheet, Text, useColorScheme, } from "react-native"; import { handleBadgeUpdate } from "."; function App(): JSX.Element { const isDarkMode = useColorScheme() === "dark"; const backgroundStyle = { backgroundColor: isDarkMode ? "white" : "black", }; async function handlePermissions() { const status = await Notifications.Push.getPermissionStatus(); if (status === "SHOULD_REQUEST") { await Notifications.Push.requestPermissions({ sound: true, badge: true, }); } if (status === "SHOULD_EXPLAIN_THEN_REQUEST") { await Notifications.Push.requestPermissions({ sound: true, badge: true, }); } } const tokenReceivedHandler = (token: string) => { console.log({ token }); }; useEffect(() => { handlePermissions(); const listener = Notifications.Push.onTokenReceived(tokenReceivedHandler); return () => listener && listener.remove(); }, []); return ( <SafeAreaView style={backgroundStyle}> { { /* rest UI*/ } } </SafeAreaView> ); } const styles = StyleSheet.create({ // styles }); export default App;

接下来，我们通过调用App.tsx文件中的enable()方法来启用推送通知。 当该组件安装时，我们运行一个效果，通过handlePermission()方法请求用户接收推送通知的权限，其中它根据现有权限状态允许的内容请求用户接收通知的权限。

如果用户接受，当我们的应用程序在操作系统解析推送通知后收到推送通知时，我们还会注册一个侦听器。 我们通过调用Notifications.Push.onTokenReceived()方法来做到这一点，该方法获取一个名为tokenReceivedHandler的回调，该回调接收推送令牌作为参数并将其打印到控制台，如下所示。

您可以配置一个 API 端点以将令牌发送到您的后端某处，但我们现在只需将其打印到控制台即可。

现在我们有了推送令牌，接下来就是使用此令牌向用户发送通知。 让我们复制推送令牌并前往我们的 AWS pinpoint 仪表板。

从 AWS Pinpoint 仪表板发送测试推送通知

在 AWS pinpoint 仪表板上，选择新创建的 pinpoint 项目。

现在让我们从 pinpoint 仪表板向我们的设备发送一个简单的推送通知。 我们将通过以下步骤执行此操作，请确保在执行此步骤之前移动应用程序已关闭或在后台运行，以便通知弹出并显示在通知抽屉中。

  1. 从侧边栏中选择测试消息。
  2. 在表单中，选择推送通知渠道。
  3. 输入目标设备token； 您可以指定多个标记，并用逗号分隔。
  4. 选择推送通知服务。 由于我们正在开发，所以我们选择了APN沙箱，这是测试期间用于发送通知的服务。
  5. 通常，您可以在发送推送通知时发送标准或原始通知。 稍后我们将在使用 AWS 开发工具包编写代码时探索原始通知。
  6. 指定消息的标题和正文。
  7. 选择用户单击通知时的操作。 我们只想打开用户的应用程序。
  8. 单击发送以发送消息。

您现在应该会看到设备或模拟器上显示的消息，如下所示，单击该消息会自动打开您的应用程序。

虽然这有效，但请记住，操作系统仅在应用程序处于后台模式或关闭时显示类似的通知。 那么如果应用程序处于活动状态或在前台会发生什么呢？ 那么，我们还可以添加一个监听器来处理这种情况。 更新您的App.tsx文件以匹配以下内容：

 import {Alert, SafeAreaView, ScrollView, StatusBar, StyleSheet, Text, useColorScheme,} from 'react-native' function App(): JSX.Element { //... useEffect(() => { handlePermissions(); const listener = Notifications.Push.onTokenReceived(myTokenReceivedHandler); Notifications.Push.onNotificationReceivedInForeground(async message => { if (message.body) { Alert.alert(message.body); } }); return () => listener && listener.remove(); }, []); return ( <SafeAreaView style={backgroundStyle}> //... </SafeAreaView>) }

现在，当应用程序位于前台时收到通知时，我们会在屏幕上收到一条警报，其消息正文如下所示。

您还可以使用此功能通知您的通知提供程序用户已收到记录指标的通知。 由于假设应用程序在运行时不在前台，因此您还可以使用它来记录指标或执行其他工作。

从代码发送推送通知

通常，在现实应用程序中，您需要从代码而不是某些自定义 UI 发送推送通知。 此外，与上面的示例不同，在大多数情况下，您希望将一些json数据附加到通知中并自定义它们以满足您的需求，添加通知徽章等。

让我们编写一个迷你 Node.js 脚本来执行此操作。

 mkdir sample cd sample npm init --y touch push.js npm install aws-sdk

更新push.js文件以匹配以下内容：

 "use strict"; const AWS = require("aws-sdk"); const region = "us-east-1"; var applicationId = "e5e878550b874d4b8148511c16873af4"; function CreateMessageRequest() { var recipientPushToken = "b6a6cafb435111685b948fad5891b57560868104a47165c4fe21e0119387dfe4" var messageRequest = { Addresses: { [recipientPushToken]: { ChannelType: "APNS_SANDBOX", }, }, MessageConfiguration: { APNSMessage: { Action:"OPEN_APP", RawContent: JSON.stringify({ name: "benzo", some_property:"yupeee!" aps: { alert: { title: "test title from notifications app", body: "app says howdy! 👋", }, sound: "default", badge: 2, }, }), }, }, }; return messageRequest; } function SendMessage() { var messageRequest = CreateMessageRequest(); AWS.config.update({ region: region }); var pinpoint = new AWS.Pinpoint(); var params = { ApplicationId: applicationId, MessageRequest: messageRequest, }; pinpoint.sendMessages(params, function (err, data) { if (err) console.log(err); else console.dir(data); }); } SendMessage();

此代码片段只是向push服务发出请求，以向某些用户的设备发送通知。 它调用sendMessage自定义函数，该函数通过调用createMessageRequest()函数创建请求，该函数返回具有两个属性的对象。
第一个属性Addresses保存了我们要将消息发送到的收件人列表，该对象中的每个键都是收件人的推送令牌，值是推送通知服务的对象。

第二个属性MessageConfiguration保存有关要发送的消息的信息。 我们指定了单击通知时打开应用程序的操作。 接下来是rawMessage属性。 还记得之前，当我们从 pinpoint 仪表板发送通知时，我们说过我们可以发送标准或原始通知。 通常，在大多数情况下，您将发送原始通知，因为它们允许您添加任何 JSON 数据，这就是我们使用rawMessage属性的原因。

首先，我们指定了一些我们希望成为消息一部分的硬编码随机属性。 我们简单地选择了name和some_property 。 最后，我们指定一个强制性的aps属性。 我们还指示系统在消息到达时播放默认通知声音以及硬编码的应用程序徽章 2，以便操作系统设置如下所示的徽章。

推送通知有效负载的最大大小为 4KB，因此请尽量保持最小。

创建该对象并从createMessageRequest函数返回后，我们将使用首选源更新 AWS 开发工具包，然后创建一个 AWS pinpoint 实例并调用其sendmessages方法，该方法将向对象提供我们的消息请求和 AWS pinpoint 应用程序ID。 如果调用成功或失败，响应将记录到控制台。

打开终端并运行以下命令：

如果消息发送成功，则会将响应打印到控制台，如下所示。

清理

为确保您的 AWS 账户中没有任何未使用的资源，如果您不打算保留在此项目中创建的所有资源，请运行以下命令来删除它们。

结论

推送通知仍然是吸引移动和网络应用程序用户的流行渠道。 本指南解释了推送通知的工作原理，并探索了将此功能集成到 IOS 应用程序中的简单方法。



原文:

Engaging customers through various communication channels is important in today's dynamic business landscape. From emails and SMS to push notifications and voice messages, staying connected with your audience is the key to thriving.

This guide focuses on the push notification channel and the logistics of sending these notifications to mobile applications. Although we will focus on IOS applications built with React Native, the concept is essentially the same for Android apps.

We will create a mini React native app and use the AWS Amplify push notification feature and its easy-to-use APIs to set up and receive push notifications in our app. Under the hood, Amplify push notifications uses AWS Pinpoint, an AWS service that would serve as our our method for segmenting and targeting users.

We will first set up and get push tokens. We will then use these tokens to send push notifications from our AWS pinpoint dashboard. Then, we will show how to do the same from a mini Node.js app using the AWS library.

Prerequisites

You need to know the following to proceed with this guide:

  • Familiarity with React and TypeScript
  • Understanding of basic mobile app development with React Native
  • Familiarity with Restful APIs and HTTP

To be able to replicate this guide locally, you need the following:

  • A MacBook with Xcode 13.4+ and/or iPhone for development.
  • An Apple developer account, enrolled in the membershipprogram.
  • An AWS account.

How Push Notification Work

Below is a diagram illustrating the main steps in sending push notifications to mobile devices. Understanding this diagram makes it easier to get started and tweak things in your app. This diagram will form the basis of all the decisions made in this guide. We will briefly explain how it works on a basic level then, based on this explanation, we will see what we need to do to build our mobile app to make this flow possible.

Let's break down how sending push notifications works in simple terms, step by step:

  1. First, the operating system (iOS in our case) asks the user if they want to receive notifications.
  2. If the user accepts, the app interfaces with the device operating system.
  3. Next, the operating system, via HTTP, requests for a "push token," — which is just a string that will be used to send notifications to the device for the mobile app. This token is gotten from the push service and is then sent back to the mobile app
  4. The app sends this token over the internet (using, for example, HTTP) to a backend web server.
  5. When the token arrives at the server, based on its configuration and preferences, it may store the token and associate it with the user's data on some database server. Whenever the server wishes to send the user a push notification, it retrieves the push token, composes the message it wants to send to the device, and passes it to the push provider (in our case, AWS pinpoint).
  6. It then modifies the configuration of the messages, includes any other credentials required to send the message, and then sends it to the push service.
  7. The push service then delivers it to the device, which is displayed on the user's device. This behavior when the notification gets to the device, depending on whether the mobile app it is intended for is open (app in foreground ) or not(the app is closed or in the background ), is handled accordingly. Later in this guide, we will see how to handle all these situations.

You may wonder why the backend, — our custom API, does not directly send the message request straight to the Push service. Why does it have to go through the push provider first?

Well, technically, the backend could send notification requests directly to the push service. However, by doing so, the backend would miss the benefits provided by the notifications provider. These advantages include analytics, tracking metrics when users receive notifications, conducting campaigns (sending messages to users at specific times), managing user journeys, and more. These features are common requirements for businesses when sending out notifications.

Development Environment

As of the time of writing, this project was tested on an Intel MacBook with macOS 13.3, Xcode 14.7, and an iPhone running IOS 16.5.

Project Setup

We will revisit our push notifications diagram later, but for now, let's create a React native project.

Run the following command in your terminal:

npx react-native@latest init notificationsapp

The above command creates a bare react native mobile app in a folder called notificationsapp. Feel free to give yours any name that suits you.

Next, let us preview this app. Remember, we will only focus on the IOS part of things. We can get push tokens from simulators on MacBooks with an M1 or M2 chip. You can directly run the app on your simulator.

Run the following command in your terminal:

If you are running on a MacBook with an Intel processor, we will need to preview on an actual device to get push tokens. Open the ios folder in your newly created project and open the .xcworkspace folder in Xcode, then follow this guide to have it up and running on your device.

You should see the app running on your device, as shown below.

Now that we have our App up and running let's update the App.tsx file with the following:

import React, { useEffect } from "react";
import {
  SafeAreaView,
  ScrollView,
  StatusBar,
  StyleSheet,
  Text,
  useColorScheme,
} from "react-native";
import { Colors } from "react-native/Libraries/NewAppScreen";
import { handleBadgeUpdate } from ".";

function App(): JSX.Element {
  const isDarkMode = useColorScheme() === "dark";
  const backgroundStyle = {
    backgroundColor: isDarkMode ? Colors.darker : Colors.lighter,
  };

  return (
    <SafeAreaView style={backgroundStyle}>
      <StatusBar
        barStyle={isDarkMode ? "light-content" : "dark-content"}
        backgroundColor={backgroundStyle.backgroundColor}
      />
      <ScrollView
        contentInsetAdjustmentBehavior="automatic"
        style={backgroundStyle}
      >
        <Text
          style={[
            styles.sectionTitle,
            {
              color: isDarkMode ? "white" : "black",
            },
          ]}
        >
          ios notifications example 😊
        </Text>
      </ScrollView>
    </SafeAreaView>
  );
}

const styles = StyleSheet.create({
  sectionTitle: {
    fontSize: 24,
    fontWeight: "600",
    marginTop: 70,
    textAlign: "center",
  },
});

export default App;

Once our app refreshes, it should look like the one in the image below.

Setting up Amplify

You need to configure the Amplify CLI locally, and connect it to your AWS account. Run the following command in your terminal:

npm install -g @aws-amplify/cli

Next, run the amplify configure command to configure your AWS Amplify environment. Follow the prompts to create a new IAM user that AWS Amplify will use to manage resources for your app. Here’s a link with step-by-step details on how to configure the Amplify CLI.

Now that the CLI is ready lets now head over to the root of our react native project and run this command:

This command will ask you to select a name for your application here, and I called mine notificationsapp. The CLI will also detect that we are developing a React native app and presents us with some default options to accept.

Once this is done, it will create amplify and src folders at the root of our project. The amplify folder holds all the configurations for all the services we will use in our project, with all the cloud formation templates required to create these resources on our AWS console.
We are only interested in the contents of the src/aws-exports.js file, which will be used later to configure the Amplify library.

You should also see a newly created amplify project on your AWS account, as shown below.

Adding Amplify Push Notifications

Next, we need to integrate the push notifications feature to our app. There are several things we need to put in place to add this functionality but first, let us take a closer look at our push notification diagram once more.

The diagram doesn't specify how our app will get installed on the user's device, so our app needs to have a provisioning profile. Xcode already created one for us to use during development; that is why we could build and run our app on an iOS device.

When you are ready to push your application to the app store, you need to create one from your developer account, but since we are only testing, we are fine with the default one created during development.

When the operating system requests a push token from the push service, the push service won’t just respond to that request and generate tokens randomly because the OS requests it. It needs to know the app and ensure it supports receiving push notifications. It needs an app or bundle id to do that.

Let's create an app id that supports push notifications and add this capability to our app from Xcode.

Create an App Id That Supports Push Notifications

Head over you your Apple Developer account and select identifiers from the page.

Click the plus icon to create a new App id of type App.

Add a description and name for your app id. Here we called ours com.notiapp.noti12345. Scroll to the bottom of the page and check push notifications in the Capabilities section.

Review all the changes, then click the Register button to register your new app id, as seen below.

You should now see a newly created app id in the identifiers section.

Now head over to your <YOUR-PROJECT-NAME>.xcworkspace file on Xcode, and in the Signings & Capabilities tap, update the bundle identifier with your newly created app id. Next, click on the + Capability button.

Next, add push notifications capability by searching for and selecting it.

Push notification capability means we also want our app to receive notifications when the app is in the background. Let's add background capabilities to our app and turn on remote notifications by searching for and selecting background mode capabilities, then checking remote notifications.

This should be all for now.

Generate an SSL Certificate That Is Bound to Our App Id

In the push notifications diagram, when the push notifications provider sends a request to the push service to send a notification to a device with the push token, the push service will not just randomly respond to message-sending requests from any random server because it could be a malicious attempt to spam or mislead users. We need to generate an SSL certificate that is bound to our app id and is known only to the push provider and the push service so that each time the push provider wants to send a message, it presents this certificate to validate the request and send the message to the targeted device.

Let's create and download a certificate signing request. Head over to your keychain from your device utilities. Create one and download the file as shown below.

Head to the Identifiers section on your Apple Developer account, and select your newly created app. In the capacities tab, scroll to push notifications and click configure, as seen below.

Under Production SSL Certificate, click the Create Certificate button.

Upload your Certificate Signing request file to create the new certificate, then download and save the resulting certificate to your local machine.

Double click on the downloaded file to install it on your keychain.

Open your keychain app and search for the installed certificate. The certificate's name will be the same as your app id's. Click on Export items from the File menu bar and export in .p12 format. Take note of the path pointing of this file, as we will use it later.

This step only applies to users developing with Intel-based MacBooks. Since these users need to test their app on an actual device to be able to get push tokens, they need to register their device on their Apple developer account to receive push notifications and get an error while developing as shown below.

Register Your Device

Head over to the Certificate, Identifiers & Profile section of your Developer Account and click on Devices from the menu. Click on the plus icon to add a device, specify the device platform, a random name of your choice, and the device UDID, then click register as shown below.

Now we can go ahead and add push notifications to our app. At the root of your project, run this command in your terminal:

amplify add notifications

In the prompt, choose to enable the Apple Push Notifications channel and then choose a certificate and specify a relative path to the certificate with a .p12 extension as shown below.

Once this is successful, the amplify folder will be updated accordingly, and our new AWS pinpoint app will be created on our dashboard as shown below

Next, let’s install the necessary libraries we will need to complete our setup. Run the following command in your terminal:

npm install aws-amplify @aws-amplify/rtn-push-notification amazon-cognito-identity-js @react-native-community/netinfo @react-native-async-storage/async-storage react-native-get-random-values react-native-url-polyfill`

Let's update our index.js file to use the react-native-get-random-values and react-native-url-polyfill modules.

import 'react-native-get-random-values';
import 'react-native-url-polyfill/auto';

Next, let's link all the installed modules to our ios directory. Run the following command at the root of your project:

If you get an error like this:

Remember, Amplify push notifications is geared to only work on iOS apps with a deployment target of at least iOS 13.0. To resolve this issue, open the ios folder in your app and update your podfile as shown below:

require_relative '../node_modules/react-native/scripts/react_native_pods'
require_relative '../node_modules/@react-native-community/cli-platform-ios/native_modules'

platform :ios, "13.0"
prepare_react_native_project!

Then in your ios folder clear existing pods by running the following command:

Finally, run this:

This should fix the issue.

Now open the <YOUR PROJECT NAME>.xcworkspace file in XCode and make the following updates.

Update the Appdeligate.m file to match the following:

#import "AppDelegate.h"

#import <React/RCTBundleURLProvider.h>
#import "AmplifyPushNotification.h"

@implementation AppDelegate


- (BOOL)application:(UIApplication *)application didFinishLaunchingWithOptions:(NSDictionary *)launchOptions
{
 self.moduleName = @"notificationsapp";
 // You can add your custom initial props in the dictionary below.
 // They will be passed down to the ViewController used by React Native.
 self.initialProps = @{};
 return [super application:application didFinishLaunchingWithOptions:launchOptions];
}

- (NSURL *)sourceURLForBridge:(RCTBridge *)bridge
{
#if DEBUG
 return [[RCTBundleURLProvider sharedSettings] jsBundleURLForBundleRoot:@"index"];
#else
 return [[NSBundle mainBundle] URLForResource:@"main" withExtension:@"jsbundle"];
#endif
}

/// This method controls whether the `concurrentRoot`feature of React18 is turned on or off.
///
/// @see: https://reactjs.org/blog/2022/03/29/react-v18.html
/// @note: This requires to be rendering on Fabric (i.e. on the New Architecture).
/// @return: `true` if the `concurrentRoot` feature is enabled. Otherwise, it returns `false`.
- (BOOL)concurrentRootEnabled
{
 return true;
}
- (void)application:(UIApplication *)application didRegisterForRemoteNotificationsWithDeviceToken:(NSData *)deviceToken {
 [AmplifyPushNotification didRegisterForRemoteNotificationsWithDeviceToken:deviceToken];
}

- (void)application:(UIApplication *)application didReceiveRemoteNotification:(NSDictionary *)userInfo fetchCompletionHandler:(void (^)(UIBackgroundFetchResult result))completionHandler {
 [AmplifyPushNotification didReceiveRemoteNotification:userInfo withCompletionHandler:completionHandler];
}
@end

This file is the root file of any iOS project. It holds the code and configurations our app uses during startup and the interactions between our app and the operating system, irrespective of whether our application is running.

We updated it with some imports from the Amplify notification model. We added two methods to handle registration for push tokens and handling incoming notifications.

Building the Frontend

So far, we focused more on the configuration part of things. We already know how push notifications work, so we will make changes to our index.js and App.tsx files and then map these changes to the steps in our push notifications diagram.

Update your index.js file to match the following:

import 'react-native-get-random-values';
import 'react-native-url-polyfill/auto';
import {Alert, AppRegistry} from 'react-native';
import App from './App';
import {name as appName} from './app.json';
import {Amplify, Notifications} from 'aws-amplify';
import awsconfig from './src/aws-exports';
Amplify.configure(awsconfig);
Notifications.Push.enable();

AppRegistry.registerComponent(appName, () => App);

Next, update your App.tsx file to match the following:

import { Notifications } from "aws-amplify";
import React, { useEffect } from "react";
import {
  Alert,
  SafeAreaView,
  ScrollView,
  StatusBar,
  StyleSheet,
  Text,
  useColorScheme,
} from "react-native";
import { handleBadgeUpdate } from ".";
function App(): JSX.Element {
  const isDarkMode = useColorScheme() === "dark";
  const backgroundStyle = {
    backgroundColor: isDarkMode ? "white" : "black",
  };

  async function handlePermissions() {
    const status = await Notifications.Push.getPermissionStatus();
    if (status === "SHOULD_REQUEST") {
      await Notifications.Push.requestPermissions({
        sound: true,
        badge: true,
      });
    }
    if (status === "SHOULD_EXPLAIN_THEN_REQUEST") {
      await Notifications.Push.requestPermissions({
        sound: true,
        badge: true,
      });
    }
  }

  const tokenReceivedHandler = (token: string) => {
    console.log({ token });
  };

  useEffect(() => {
    handlePermissions();
    const listener = Notifications.Push.onTokenReceived(tokenReceivedHandler);
    return () => listener && listener.remove();
  }, []);

  return (
    <SafeAreaView style={backgroundStyle}>
      {
        {
          /* rest UI*/
        }
      }
    </SafeAreaView>
  );
}
const styles = StyleSheet.create({
  // styles
});
export default App;

The index.js file starts by using the amplify configuration in the src/aws-exports.js file to configure the amplify library. Next, we enable push notifications by calling the enable() method next in our App.tsx file. When this component mounts, we run an effect that requests permission from the user to receive push notifications via the handlePermission() method, where it requests the user's permission to receive notifications based on what the existing permission status allows.

If the user accepts, we also register a listener when our app receives a push notification after the operating system resolves it. We do this by calling the Notifications.Push.onTokenReceived() method, which gets fed a callback called tokenReceivedHandler, which receives the push token as a parameter and prints it to the console as shown below.

You could configure an API endpoint to send the token to your backend somewhere, but we are fine with just printing it to our console for now.

Now that we have the push token, the next thing is to use this token to send users notifications. Let's copy the push token and head over to our AWS pinpoint dashboard.

Sending Test Push Notifications from AWS Pinpoint Dashboard

On your AWS pinpoint dashboard, select your newly created pinpoint project.

Let's now send a simple push notification to our device from our pinpoint dashboard. We will do so via the following steps, ensure that the mobile app is closed or in the background before you go through this step so that the notification will pop up and appear in the notification drawer.

  1. Select test messaging from the sidebar.
  2. In the form, select the push notification channel.
  3. Input the target device token; you can specify multiple tokens separated by a comma.
  4. Select the push notification service. Since we are developing, we selected APN sandbox, which is the service used to send notifications during testing.
  5. Usually, you can send standard or raw notifications when sending push notifications. We will explore raw notifications when we write code using the AWS SDK later.
  6. Specify the title and body of the message.
  7. Select an action when the user clicks on the notification. We just want to open the users' app.
  8. Click send to send the message.

You should now see the message displayed on your device or emulator, as shown below, and clicking on the message automatically opens your App.

While this works, remember that the OS only displays a notification like this when the App is in background mode or closed. So what happens if the App is active or in the foreground? Well, we can also add a listener to handle this situation. Update your App.tsx file to match the following:

import {Alert,   SafeAreaView,
  ScrollView,
  StatusBar,
  StyleSheet,
  Text,
  useColorScheme,} from 'react-native'

function App(): JSX.Element {
//...

 useEffect(() => {
    handlePermissions();
    const listener = Notifications.Push.onTokenReceived(myTokenReceivedHandler);
    Notifications.Push.onNotificationReceivedInForeground(async message => {
      if (message.body) {
        Alert.alert(message.body);
      }
    });
    return () => listener && listener.remove();
  }, []);

return 
  ( <SafeAreaView style={backgroundStyle}>
      //...
   </SafeAreaView>)
}

Now, when we get a notification while the App is in the foreground, we get an alert on our screen with the body of the message as shown below.

You can also use this function to inform your notification provider that a user has received a notification to record metrics. Amplify push also provides a Notifications.Push.onNotificationReceivedInBackground method you can register in your index.js file, not **** in an app component. Since it is assumed that the app is not in the foreground when running, you can also use this to log metrics or do other work.

Sending Push Notifications from Code

Typically, in real-world apps, you will want to send push notifications from your code, not from some custom UI. Also, unlike the example above, in most cases, you want to attach some json data to your notifications and customize them to meet your needs, add notification badges, etc.

Let's write a mini Node.js script to do that.

mkdir sample
cd sample
npm init --y
touch push.js
npm install aws-sdk

Update the push.js file to match the following:

"use strict";
const AWS = require("aws-sdk");
const region = "us-east-1";
var applicationId = "e5e878550b874d4b8148511c16873af4";

function CreateMessageRequest() {
    var recipientPushToken =  "b6a6cafb435111685b948fad5891b57560868104a47165c4fe21e0119387dfe4"
    var messageRequest = {
        Addresses: {
            [recipientPushToken]: {
                ChannelType: "APNS_SANDBOX",
            },
        },
        MessageConfiguration: {
            APNSMessage: {
                Action:"OPEN_APP",
                RawContent: JSON.stringify({
                    name: "benzo",
                    some_property:"yupeee!"
                    aps: {
                        alert: {
                            title: "test title from notifications app",
                            body: "app says howdy! 👋",
                        },
                        sound: "default",
                        badge: 2,
                    },
                }),
            },
        },
    };
    return messageRequest;
}

function SendMessage() {
    var messageRequest = CreateMessageRequest();
    AWS.config.update({ region: region });
    var pinpoint = new AWS.Pinpoint();
    var params = {
        ApplicationId: applicationId,
        MessageRequest: messageRequest,
    };
    pinpoint.sendMessages(params, function (err, data) {
        if (err) console.log(err);
        else console.dir(data);
    });
}

SendMessage();

This snippet just makes a request to the push service to send a notification to some user's device. It calls the sendMessage custom function, which creates a request by invoking the createMessageRequest() function, which returns an object with two properties.
The first property, Addresses holds the list of recipients we want to send the message to, and each key in this object is the recipient's push token, and the value is an object for the push notification service.

The second property, MessageConfiguration holds information about the message to be sent. We specified an action to open the app when the notification is clicked. Next is the rawMessage property. Remember earlier, when we sent a notification from our pinpoint dashboard, we said we could send standard or raw notifications. Typically, in most cases, you will send raw notifications since they allow you to add any JSON data, and that's why we used the rawMessage property.

First, we specified some hardcoded random properties we wanted to be part of the message. We trivially chose name and some_property. Finally, we specify a mandatory aps property. We also instruct the system to play the default notification sound when the message arrives and a hardcoded app badge of 2 so that the operating system sets a badge like the one shown below.

The maximum size of a push notification’s payload is 4KB, so try to keep things minimal.

Once this object is created and returned from the createMessageRequest function, we update the AWS SDK with our preferred origin, after which we create an AWS pinpoint instance and invoke its sendmessages method, which gets fed an object with our message request and our AWS pinpoint app id. The response is logged to the console if the call succeeds or fails.

Open your terminal and run the following:

If the message was sent successfully, a response is printed to the console, like the one below.

Clean Up

To ensure that you don’t have any unused resources in you AWS account, run the following command to delete all the resources that were created in this project if you don’t intend to keep them.

Conclusion

Push notifications continue to remain a popular channel to engage users on mobile and web applications. This guide explains how push notifications work and explores an easy way to integrate this feature in an IOS app.
Items.Author: codebeast

Items.Title: 带有源代码的类似 Facebook 的克隆 | FaceBook Like Clone With Source Code
Items.Description:
一旦被暂停，brojenuel 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，brojenuel 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，brojenuel 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己才能访问。

如果 brojenuel 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 brojenuel：

使 brojenuel 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

坚持不懈的 brojenuel 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Once suspended, brojenuel will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, brojenuel will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by brojenuel will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If brojenuel is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag brojenuel:

Make all posts by brojenuel less visible

brojenuel consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging brojenuel will restore default visibility to their posts.
Items.Author: brojenuel

Items.Title: CodeCatalyst 的真实项目 - 我们的黑客马拉松让我们深入了解什么有效、什么无效 | A real project with CodeCatalyst - Our hackathon gave us a good insight into what works and what doesn't
Items.Description:
正如 Matt 在他的项目介绍文章中所写，我们一直在使用Amazon CodeCatalyst来处理我们的所有项目活动。 这很好地表明了 CodeCatalyst 已经“ 准备好迎接黄金时段”，以及需要进行进一步的调整，以便将该服务发展为成熟的、全有或全无的集成 DevOps 服务。
推动使用 CodeCatalyst 的人是我自己，因为我想在更大的团队和实际项目中尝试一下新的 Amazon 服务，该服务最近在 re:invent 2023 上宣布后进入“GA”状态。 这很有趣——我们也学到了很多东西！
让我们看一些细节。

我们如何使用“问题”来计划我们的任务

我们使用 CodeCatalyst 的旅程始于使用 CodeCatalyst 的“问题”组件进行的非常敏捷且紧张的规划会议。 考虑到黑客马拉松的性质，我们在系统中创建了大约前十个问题。 我们三个人是 CodeCatalyst 的“新用户”，但他们能够快速与问题组件交互 - 默认情况下，这是一个简单的看板，并且具有结构良好的用户界面。 我们直接错过的一件事是能够将图像复制/粘贴到问题内的评论中......并将文件附加到问题本身。 目前将问题相互联系起来也几乎是不可能的。
我们使用简单的工作流程并进行定期计划（每周两次），并在不到四个星期的时间内创建/更新/关闭了 50 多个问题（部分范围非常小）。 总的来说，可用的功能对于我们的项目来说足够好并且支持我们的项目。

协作源代码 - 使用 Git 存储库和拉取请求

我们四个人都是来自底层的（社区）构建者，我们所需要的只是一个可以将代码推送到的 Git 存储库:-)
当然这不是正确的，但 CodeCatalyst 允许您托管自己的私有 Git 存储库。 每个项目可以有多个存储库。 在我们的例子中，我们决定采用单一存储库方法，因此我们严格在单个存储库中工作。 使用源代码存储库“很好”并且按预期工作。 我们还决定尽早并尽可能频繁地合并到我们的“主”分支。 然而，团队在设置 Pull 请求以及在审查 Pull 请求时在 UI 中工作时遇到了困难。 最有问题的事情：UI 响应时间、处理拉取请求的评论（例如线程评论）以及创建拉取请求。 整个 UI 还不像其他竞争对手（例如 Github）那样“结构化” - 示例：创建 PR 时的 PR 标题/描述建议、允许您创建 PR 的终端/命令行中的链接。

我们错过的另一件事是在线“git Blame” - 不是互相指责，而是了解是什么改变并破坏了我们的“黑客”“黑客马拉松”源代码:-)


酷：名为 *.md 的文件的 Markdown 渲染。 这里缺少：不可能包含本地图像。

使用工作流程 - Github 救援行动！

我们开始使用 Amazon CodeCatalyst中原生可用的操作构建工作流程 - 使用“cdk 部署”操作来部署基础设施，使用“s3 部署”操作来部署我们的前端 - 但很快就放弃了这种做法，转而使用 Github工作流程中的操作允许将我们的 Flutter 应用程序构建为 CDK 资产。 随着我们的持续集成部分在npm实现中日趋成熟，不再有理由返回到本机操作，因为 CI 部分是通过 npm 处理的。 我们当前的工作流程和 CI/CD 管道：

这里的主要偏好是允许持续部署到我们的生产环境。 这将经历初始“沙箱”部署，升级到“测试”环境，然后部署到“生产”环境。 管道已准备好执行集成测试，但尚未实现 - 这是 _hack_athon 的本质:-)

这肯定是接下来要改变的事情之一——添加真正的集成测试并添加一些安全验证。

正如我所提到的，我们的工作流程非常基本 - 这里是部署到我们的沙箱环境之前的摘录：

 BuildAndTestCDKApp: Identifier: aws/github-actions-runner@v1 Compute: Type: EC2 Inputs: Sources: - WorkflowSource Variables: - Name: AWS_REGION Value: us-east-1 - Name: NODE_ENV Value: sandbox Outputs: AutoDiscoverReports: Enabled: true ReportNamePrefix: rpt IncludePaths: - ./coverage/lcov.info Configuration: Steps: - name: Flutter Build Web uses: subosito/flutter-action@v2.8.0 with: channel: stable - name: Java Install uses: actions/setup-java@v2 with: distribution: zulu java-version: "11" - name: Setup Android SDK uses: android-actions/setup-android@v2 - uses: actions/setup-node@v3 with: node-version: 16 - run: npm ci - run: npm run - run: npm run synth Environment: Connections: - Role: CodeCatalystWorkflowDevelopmentRole-AWSCommunityBuilders Name: community-builders-sandbox Name: speakers_sandbox DeploySandboxCDKApp: DependsOn: - BuildAndTestCDKApp Actions: DeployBase: Identifier: aws/github-actions-runner@v1 Compute: Type: EC2 Inputs: Sources: - WorkflowSource Variables: - Name: AWS_REGION Value: us-east-1 - Name: NODE_ENV Value: sandbox Configuration: Steps: - name: Flutter Build Web uses: subosito/flutter-action@v2.8.0 with: channel: stable - name: Java Install uses: actions/setup-java@v2 with: distribution: zulu java-version: "11" - name: Setup Android SDK uses: android-actions/setup-android@v2 - uses: actions/setup-node@v3 with: node-version: 16 - run: npm ci - run: npm run deploy Environment: Connections: - Role: CodeCatalystWorkflowDevelopmentRole-AWSCommunityBuilders Name: community-builders-sandbox Name: speakers_sandbox IntegrationTestSandbox: Identifier: aws/build@v1 Inputs: Sources: - WorkflowSource Configuration: Steps: - Run: ls -al Compute: Type: Lambda DependsOn: - DeploySandboxCDKApp

正如您所看到的，许多 CD（以及“部署”）在package.json中相应的任务定义中运行。

没有“开源”或“只读”视图

我们真的很乐意开源我们的整个项目，让您有机会参与并从我们构建的内容中学习，但不幸的是 Amazon CodeCatalyst 目前不提供开源或“只读”视图，并迫使您创建一个CodeCatalyst 的帐户 - 这些事情让我们感到困难，因为我们无法链接到我们的来源。
如果您是AWS 社区构建者，请在 Slack 中联系我们，我们可以为您提供该项目的访问权限。

我们在工作流程组件中真正错过了什么

  • 在升级到我们的“生产”环境之前进行手动批准
  • 来自工作流程的通知 - 本来可以通过此实现来完成，但我们没有花时间来实现
  • 集成通过“npm - cdk 部署”部署的操作，使基础设施中的更改可见
  • 将现有管道或渲染 CDK 管道导入 CodeCatalyst 工作流程 工作流程的“杀手级功能”（至少就我们而言）是可以使用 Github 操作作为工作流程的一部分。 只需进行微小的调整，即可将现有的 Github 工作流程转换为 CodeCatalyst 工作流程。 如果你们知道有一个现有工具可以自动将现有的 Github 工作流程转换为 CodeCatalyst 工作流程 - 请告诉我。

隐藏的宝石：开发环境

本质上，它是一个类似于 Gitpod 的“服务中的服务”，您可以在其中托管 IDE 或开发环境。 在我们的项目中，我们都没有使用它，因为架构很小，而且我们只有这个项目。 此外，我们主要在我们的主机上进行开发，而不会经常在机器之间切换。 这就是为什么我们都没有考虑在这个项目中真正使用 DevEnvironments。 最后但并非最不重要的一点是，开发环境本身并不支持 Flutter，这将迫使我们准备一个包含 Flutter 依赖项的开发文件 - 这会花费项目太多时间。
尽管如此，CodeCatalyst 中的 De 环境仍然是隐藏的瑰宝，我相信它们应该比现在更受关注。

总结我们认为有效和无效的方法以及我们最大的愿望

对于像这样的黑客马拉松（3-6 周的工作时间）来说，对于以“新”或“新鲜”代码库开始的项目来说，CodeCatalyst 似乎是一个非常好的选择。 该工具满足了大部分最低要求，以实现简单的项目管理以及与 AWS 的良好集成。
它还使我们能够快速开始并就我们的资源进行协作。

我们确实错过了工作流程通知以及工作流程中的手动审批。
随着该工具的成熟，我个人希望越来越多的 AWS 内部团队将使用 CodeCatalyst 来开发、规划和部署其内部 AWS 服务，这样用户界面中的“流程”也肯定会得到改进 - 就像今天一样真正的开发人员工作流程中存在一些问题。

最后但并非最不重要的一点 - 我们很乐意与您分享这个项目，但鉴于 CodeCatalyst 不允许“开源”项目，这是不可能的:-)

也许是下一次黑客马拉松的一些东西？

如果您想参与此项目并帮助我们塑造和构建我们的AWS 演讲者目录，以便AWS 用户组负责人更轻松地找到演讲者 - 请与我们四位中的任何一位联系以了解合作事宜！

该联系谁？
丹妮尔、马特、朱利安或我自己。

安德烈亚斯·伯格斯特罗姆 - 5 月 24 日

一旦暂停，aws-builders 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到其暂停被取消。

一旦取消暂停，aws-builders 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，aws-builders 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 aws-builders 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从仪表板重新发布帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保证 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 aws-builders：

使 aws-builders 的所有帖子不那么明显

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 aws-builders 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

As Matt already wrote in his project introduction post we have been using Amazon CodeCatalyst to handle all of our project activities. This gives a very good indication into where CodeCatalyst is already "ready for prime time" and where further adjustments need to be made in order to develop the service into a fully fledge, all-or-nothing, integrated DevOps service.
The one pushing for using CodeCatalyst was myself, as I wanted to try out the new Amazon service that recently went "GA" after being announced at re:invent 2023 in a bigger team and in a real project. It was great fun - and we learned a lot, too!
Let's look at some details.

How we planned our tasks using "issues"

Our journey with CodeCatalyst started with a - very agile and intense - planning session using the "issues" component of CodeCatalyst. Given the nature of an hackathon, we created roughly the first ten issues in the system. Three of us were "new users" for CodeCatalyst, but they were able to quickly interact with the issues component - by default, this is a simple Kanban board and has a well structured user interface. One thing we directly missed was the ability to copy/paste images into comments within the issues...and to attach files to the issues themselves. Linking issues to one another is also only hardly possible right now.
We used a simple workflow and did a regular planning (twice per week) and created/updated/closed more than 50 issues (of partly very small scope) throught the course of less than four weeks. Overall, the available functionalities were good enough for our project and supported our project.

Collaborating source code - working with Git repositories and pull requests

The four of us are (Community) Builders from the bottom and all we need to be happy is a Git repository that we can push code to :-)
Of course this is not currect, but CodeCatalyst allows you to host you own, private Git repositories. You can have multiple repositories per project. In our case, we had decided to go with a mono-repo approach and so we strickly worked in a single repository. Working with the source code repository was "just fine" and working as expected. We also decided to merge as early and as often as possible to our "main" branch. The team however struggled in working with setting up Pull Requests and working in the UI when reviewing pull requests. The most problematic things: UI response time, working with comments on pull requests (e.g. threaded comments) and creating pull requests. The whole UI does not yet feel as "structured" as in other competitors (e.g. Github) - examples: PR title/description proposals when creating a PR, link in terminal/commandline that allows your to create a PR.

Another thing we missed is "git blame" online - not to blame each other but to understand what changed and broke our "hacked" "hackathon" source code :-)


Cool: the markdown rendering for files named *.md. Missing here: including local images is not possible.

Working with Workflows - Github actions for the rescue!

We started off building our workflows with natively available actions in Amazon CodeCatalyst - using the "cdk deploy" action to deploy out infrastructure and the "s3 deploy" action to deploy our front end - but quickly shifted away from that and switched over to using Github Actions within the workflow to allow building our Flutter application as a CDK asset. As our Continuous Integration pieces matured within our npm implementation, there was less reason to go back to the native actions as the CI part was handled through npm. Our current workflow and CI/CD pipeline:

The main preference here is to allow a continuous deployment to our production environment. This goes through an intial "sandbox" deployment, promotes to a "test" environment and then deploys to "production". The pipeline is prepared to execute integration tests, but they are not yet implemented - which is the nature of a _hack_athon :-)

This would definately one of the next things to change - adding real integration tests and also adding some security verifications.

Our workflow is, as I mentioned, pretty basic - here an excerpt of it until the deployment to our sandbox environment:

BuildAndTestCDKApp: Identifier: aws/github-actions-runner@v1 Compute: Type: EC2 Inputs: Sources: - WorkflowSource Variables: - Name: AWS_REGION Value: us-east-1 - Name: NODE_ENV Value: sandbox Outputs: AutoDiscoverReports: Enabled: true ReportNamePrefix: rpt IncludePaths: - ./coverage/lcov.info Configuration: Steps: - name: Flutter Build Web uses: subosito/flutter-action@v2.8.0 with: channel: stable - name: Java Install uses: actions/setup-java@v2 with: distribution: zulu java-version: "11" - name: Setup Android SDK uses: android-actions/setup-android@v2 - uses: actions/setup-node@v3 with: node-version: 16 - run: npm ci - run: npm run - run: npm run synth Environment: Connections: - Role: CodeCatalystWorkflowDevelopmentRole-AWSCommunityBuilders Name: community-builders-sandbox Name: speakers_sandbox DeploySandboxCDKApp: DependsOn: - BuildAndTestCDKApp Actions: DeployBase: Identifier: aws/github-actions-runner@v1 Compute: Type: EC2 Inputs: Sources: - WorkflowSource Variables: - Name: AWS_REGION Value: us-east-1 - Name: NODE_ENV Value: sandbox Configuration: Steps: - name: Flutter Build Web uses: subosito/flutter-action@v2.8.0 with: channel: stable - name: Java Install uses: actions/setup-java@v2 with: distribution: zulu java-version: "11" - name: Setup Android SDK uses: android-actions/setup-android@v2 - uses: actions/setup-node@v3 with: node-version: 16 - run: npm ci - run: npm run deploy Environment: Connections: - Role: CodeCatalystWorkflowDevelopmentRole-AWSCommunityBuilders Name: community-builders-sandbox Name: speakers_sandbox IntegrationTestSandbox: Identifier: aws/build@v1 Inputs: Sources: - WorkflowSource Configuration: Steps: - Run: ls -al Compute: Type: Lambda DependsOn: - DeploySandboxCDKApp

As you can see, a lot of the CD (and also the "deployment") runs within the corresponding task definitions in package.json.

No "Open Source" or "Readonly" view

We would really love to open-source our whole projet, to give you the possibility to get involved and learn from what we build, but unfortunately Amazon CodeCatalyst does currrently not offer an Open Source or a "Readonly" view and forces you to create an account for CodeCatalyst - these are things that make it difficult for us as we cannot link to our sources.
If you are an AWS Community Builder, please reach out to either of us in Slack and we can give you access to the project.

What did we really miss in the workflow component

  • manual approvals before promotion to our "production" environment
  • Notifications from workflows - could have been done through this implementation but we did not take time to implement that
  • Integration of actions deployed through "npm - cdk deploy" with making the changes in infrastructure visible
  • importing existing pipelines or render CDK pipelines to CodeCatalyst workflows The "killer feature" of the workflows, at least as far as we see it, is the possibility to use Github actions as part of the workflow. With only minor adjustmends an existing Github workflow can be transformed to a CodeCatalyst workflow. If either of you know about an existing tool that converts existing Github workflows to CodeCatalyst workflows in an automated way - please let me know.

The hidden gem: Development Environments

One of the "hidden gems" of CodeCatalyst are the Dev Environments - also known as Development Enviroments. Essentially, its a "service in the service" similar to Gitpod where you can host your IDE or the environment that you develop on. In our project we all didn't use it as the architecture was small and we only had this single project. Also, we were developing mainly on our main machines without switching between machines too often. That's why we all did not consider really using the DevEnvironments for this project. Last but not least, Flutter is not natively supported on the Dev Environments which would have forced us to prepare a devfile that includes the Flutter dependencies - and thats something that would have taken too much time out of the project.
Still, the De Environments within CodeCatalyst are hidden gems that I believe should get more traction than they are getting until now.

A summary of what we think works and what doesn't - and our biggest wishes

For a hackathon like this (3-6 weeks working time) CodeCatalyst seems to be a very good choice for a project that starts with a "new" or "fresh" codebase. The tool has most of the minimal requirements implemented to allow simple project management and good integration with AWS.
It also allowed us to quickly get started and to collaborate on our sources.

We really missed workflow notifications as well as manual approvals in the workflows.
As the tool matures I personally hope that more and more AWS internal teams will be using CodeCatalyst to develop, plan and deploy out their internal AWS services and with that the "flow" in the User Interface will definately be improved, too - as today there are some hickups in a real developers workflow.

And, last but not least - we would love to share this project with you, but given CodeCatalyst does not allow to "Open Source" a project, that's not possible :-)

Maybe something for the next hackathon?

If you would like to get involved in this project and help us to shape and build our AWS Speakers directory to make it easier for AWS User Group Leaders to find speakers - please reach out to either of us four about collaborating!

Who to reach out to?
Danielle, Matt, Julian or myself.

Andreas Bergström - May 24

Once suspended, aws-builders will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, aws-builders will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by aws-builders will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If aws-builders is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag aws-builders:

Make all posts by aws-builders less visible

aws-builders consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging aws-builders will restore default visibility to their posts.
Items.Author: lockhead

Items.Title: 2023 年 30 强自动化测试工具：自动化测试工具入门 | Top 30 Automation Testing Tools in 2023: Getting Started with Automation Testing Tools
Items.Description:
布拉文·瓦斯克

发表于 6 月 29 日

本文是2023年排名前30的自动化测试工具的终极指南。自动化测试是使用软件自动执行测试的过程，无需任何人工干预。 这可以节省时间和金钱，还可以通过在开发过程的早期发现错误来帮助提高软件质量。

有许多不同的自动化测试工具可用，每种工具都有其优点和缺点。 在本文中，我们将了解 2023 年 30 个顶级自动化测试工具。我们将讨论每个工具的功能、优缺点以及如何在 DevOps 中使用它。

它提供了一个自动化 Web 浏览器的框架，允许测试人员和开发人员使用各种编程语言（包括 Java、Python、C# 等）编写测试脚本。 要在DevOps中应用和使用Selenium，可以按照以下步骤操作：

  1. 设置和配置：安装必要的依赖项，包括 Selenium WebDriver、特定于您要测试的浏览器的浏览器驱动程序，以及测试脚本开发所需的任何特定于编程语言的库或框架。

  2. 测试脚本开发：使用您喜欢的编程语言和测试框架，使用 Selenium WebDriver API 编写测试脚本。 该 API 提供了一组方法和命令来与 Web 元素交互、模拟用户操作并验证预期结果。

  3. 测试执行：通过使用 WebDriver 连接到浏览器来执行 Selenium 测试脚本。 与持续集成 (CI) 工具集成：将 Selenium 与流行的 CI 工具（例如 Jenkins、Bamboo 或 TeamCity）集成，以实现自动化测试执行作为 DevOps 管道的一部分。 这可确保每次代码更改或部署时自动触发测试，从而提供有关应用程序稳定性的快速反馈。

硒的特点：

  1. 跨浏览器兼容性：Selenium 支持多种浏览器，允许您在不同的浏览器上执行测试，并确保您的 Web 应用程序在这些浏览器上一致地工作。
  2. 多语言支持：Selenium 提供对各种编程语言的支持，使您能够用您选择的语言编写测试脚本。
  3. 元素识别：Selenium 提供了各种方法来定位 Web 元素并与之交互，例如 XPath、CSS 选择器和元素 ID，从而可以在测试执行期间轻松导航和操作网页。
  4. 数据驱动测试：Selenium 允许您通过集成来自外部源（例如 Excel 或数据库）的测试数据来执行数据驱动测试，从而能够使用不同的输入执行多个测试场景。
  5. 与测试框架集成：Selenium 可以轻松与 TestNG 和 JUnit 等流行测试框架集成，从而实现高级测试管理、并行测试执行和测试报告功能。
  6. 页面对象模型（POM） ：Selenium 促进了页面对象模型设计模式的使用，该模式通过将网页元素及其相关操作封装在单独的类中来帮助创建可重用和可维护的测试脚本。
  7. 可扩展性：Selenium 的开源特性允许轻松扩展以及与其他工具或框架集成，以增强测试能力。

使用 Selenium 进行 Web 自动化测试的优点：

  1. 开源且活跃的社区：Selenium 是一款开源工具，拥有大型且活跃的社区，提供持续更新、错误修复和社区支持。
  2. 跨浏览器和跨平台测试：Selenium 支持多种浏览器和平台，允许您跨各种环境测试 Web 应用程序。
  3. 支持各种编程语言：Selenium 支持多种编程语言，使您可以灵活地选择最适合您团队专业知识的语言。
  4. 与 CI 工具集成：Selenium 与流行的 CI 工具无缝集成，使自动化测试执行成为 DevOps 管道的一部分。

使用硒的缺点：

  1. 复杂的设置和配置：设置 Selenium 和管理浏览器驱动程序可能具有挑战性，特别是对于初学者或处理多个浏览器版本时。
  2. 对非 Web 应用程序的有限支持：Selenium 主要专注于 Web 应用程序测试，可能不适合自动化非 Web 应用程序。
  3. 不稳定和同步问题：Selenium 测试有时可能会面临同步问题，需要额外的努力来处理动态 Web 元素并等待页面加载或 AJAX 请求完成。
  4. 缺乏内置报告和测试管理：Selenium 不提供内置报告或测试管理功能。 您可能需要将其与其他工具或框架集成以生成全面的测试报告并管理测试执行。

它提供了用户友好的界面和丰富的功能，适合初学者和经验丰富的测试人员。 要在DevOps中应用和使用Katalon Studio，您可以按照以下步骤操作：

  1. 安装和设置：在您的计算机上下载并安装 Katalon Studio。 它附带了所有必要的依赖项，包括 WebDriver 和 Appium 驱动程序，因此您无需单独安装它们。

  2. 项目创建：在 Katalon Studio 中创建一个新项目，作为测试工件的容器。 您可以在项目结构中组织测试用例、测试套件和测试数据。

  3. 测试脚本开发：使用 Katalon Studio 直观且用户友好的界面来创建测试用例。 您可以利用 Katalon Studio 提供的内置关键字和操作，或使用 Groovy（Katalon Studio 支持的脚本语言）编写自定义代码。

  4. 测试执行：在 Katalon Studio 中执行测试用例，可以手动执行，也可以安排它们在特定时间或事件运行。 与持续集成 (CI) 工具集成：Katalon Studio 与 Jenkins、Bamboo 或 Azure DevOps 等流行的 CI 工具无缝集成，允许您将自动化测试执行合并为 DevOps 管道的一部分。 这确保了对应用程序质量的持续测试和快速反馈。

卡塔隆工作室的特点：

  1. 跨平台和跨浏览器支持：Katalon Studio 支持跨多个浏览器（Chrome、Firefox、Safari 等）和平台（Windows、macOS、Linux）测试 Web 应用程序。 它还支持使用 Appium 框架在 Android 和 iOS 设备上进行移动测试。

  2. 测试记录和回放：Katalon Studio 提供了强大的记录和回放功能，允许测试人员通过捕获与被测应用程序的交互来快速创建测试脚本。 此功能对于那些编程知识有限的人特别有用。

  3. 对象存储库：Katalon Studio 提供了一个集中的对象存储库，测试人员可以在其中存储和管理 Web 和移动元素。 这提高了可重用性并使测试维护更易于管理。

  4. 数据驱动测试：Katalon Studio 支持数据驱动测试，允许测试人员使用不同的测试数据集驱动测试用例。 测试数据可以从 Excel、CSV 或数据库等各种来源导入。

  5. 内置测试报告：Katalon Studio 生成详细的测试报告，提供对测试执行结果的全面见解。 报告包括测试摘要、日志、屏幕截图和其他相关信息，有助于测试分析和调试。

  6. 与源代码控制集成：Katalon Studio 与 Git 等流行的源代码控制系统无缝集成，从而实现团队成员之间的版本控制和协作。

使用 Katalon Studio 的优点：

  1. 一体化测试解决方案：Katalon Studio 将 Web、移动和 API 测试功能结合在一个工具中，无需单独的工具或框架。
  2. 用户友好的界面：Katalon Studio 提供了用户友好的界面，技术和非技术用户都可以使用它。 记录和回放功能进一步简化了测试脚本的创建。
  3. 丰富的功能：Katalon Studio 提供了广泛的功能，包括测试用例管理、数据驱动测试、测试执行调度和内置报告，增强了整体测试体验。
  4. 活跃的社区支持：Katalon Studio 拥有一个活跃的社区，用户可以在其中寻求帮助、分享经验并访问各种资源，包括教程、论坛和插件。

使用 Katalon Studio 的缺点：

  1. 有限的定制：虽然 Katalon Studio 提供了丰富的功能，但可能存在某些不易获得或需要额外脚本编写的定制选项或高级功能。
  2. 对GUI交互的依赖：由于Katalon Studio严重依赖GUI交互，应用程序UI的任何变化都可能影响测试脚本的稳定性，需要调整和维护。
  3. 高级功能的学习曲线：虽然 Katalon Studio 易于使用，但掌握其高级功能（例如自定义脚本）可能需要一些学习和练习。

它专注于提供快速、可靠且易于使用的测试体验。 赛普拉斯提供了直接在浏览器中运行的独特架构，允许实时交互式测试和调试。

要在DevOps中应用和使用Cypress，您可以按照以下步骤操作：

  1. 安装和设置：使用 npm（节点包管理器）安装 Cypress 并设置必要的依赖项。 项目初始化：在您所需的目录中初始化一个新的 Cypress 项目。 这将设置赛普拉斯测试自动化所需的项目结构和配置文件。

  2. 测试脚本开发：使用赛普拉斯基于 JavaScript 的富有表现力的 API 创建测试脚本。 测试执行：使用 Cypress Test Runner 执行 Cypress 测试脚本。 测试运行器提供了一个交互式 GUI，您可以在其中实时查看测试结果、调试失败并监控测试运行。

  3. 与持续集成 (CI) 工具集成：Cypress 可以通过将其配置为在无头模式下运行测试来集成到您的 DevOps 管道中，从而允许非交互式执行。 将 Cypress 与 Jenkins、Travis CI 或 GitLab CI 等流行的 CI 工具集成，以自动执行每次代码更改或部署的测试。

赛普拉斯的特点：

  1. 快速可靠：赛普拉斯的架构通过直接在浏览器中运行并密切控制被测应用程序来实现快速测试执行。 它消除了不必要的网络请求和等待，从而获得快速可靠的测试结果。

  2. 实时交互式测试：赛普拉斯提供了独特的测试运行器界面，允许实时交互式测试和调试。 您可以直接在浏览器中观察应用程序状态、执行单独的测试以及调试故障。

  3. 自动等待和重试：赛普拉斯会自动等待命令和断言完成，然后再继续下一步。 它还可以智能地重试失败的断言，使测试更加稳健和有弹性。

  4. 时间旅行：赛普拉斯记录应用程序中发生的每个操作，允许您“时间旅行”并在测试执行期间的任何时刻与应用程序交互。 此功能有助于调试和了解应用程序在特定测试步骤的状态。

  5. 自动重新加载：当发生更改时，赛普拉斯会自动重新加载被测应用程序，从而在测试开发期间提供即时反馈。

  6. 轻松调试：赛普拉斯的交互式测试运行界面及其丰富的日志记录和错误消息通过提供有关测试失败的详细信息来简化调试过程。

使用赛普拉斯的优点：

  1. 简单直观：赛普拉斯提供易于理解的 API 和用户友好的测试运行器界面，使初学者和经验丰富的测试人员都可以使用它。
  2. 快速测试执行：赛普拉斯的架构可实现快速测试执行，从而缩短总体测试时间。
  3. 全面的断言和命令：赛普拉斯提供了一组丰富的命令和断言，涵盖了大多数测试场景，减少了对外部库或插件的需求。
  4. 自动等待和重试：赛普拉斯自动处理等待和重试，增强测试稳定性并减少显式等待或睡眠语句的需要。

使用赛普拉斯的缺点：

  1. 有限的浏览器支持：Cypress 主要专注于现代 Web 浏览器，可能不支持较旧的浏览器版本或不太常见的浏览器。
  2. 不支持非 Web 应用程序：Cypress 专为 Web 应用程序测试而设计，不支持本机移动应用程序或桌面应用程序的测试。

它允许您使用单个 API 在不同的移动平台（包括 iOS、Android 和 Windows）上自动进行测试。 要在DevOps中应用和使用Appium，您可以按照以下步骤操作：

  1. 环境设置：安装必要的依赖项，包括Appium服务器、移动设备模拟器或模拟器以及目标移动平台必要的SDK。

  2. 测试脚本开发：使用您喜欢的编程语言和 Appium 客户端库编写测试脚本。 利用可用的 API 方法与移动元素交互、模拟用户操作并执行断言。

  3. 测试执行：通过连接到 Appium 服务器和目标移动设备或模拟器/模拟器来执行 Appium 测试脚本。 与持续集成 (CI) 工具集成：将 Appium 与 CI 工具（例如 Jenkins、Bamboo 或 Azure DevOps）集成，以自动执行移动测试作为 DevOps 管道的一部分。 这确保了对移动应用程序质量的持续测试和反馈。

跨平台支持：Appium 允许您编写一次测试脚本并在多个平台上运行它们，包括 iOS、Android 和 Windows。 这节省了为不同平台维护单独的测试脚本的时间和精力。

  • 支持本机、混合和 Web 应用程序：Appium 支持测试本机移动应用程序（使用特定于平台的语言和框架构建）、混合应用程序（Web 和本机元素的组合）和 Web 应用程序（移动 Web 应用程序）。 这种多功能性使 Appium 适用于各种移动应用程序。

  • 广泛的编程语言支持：Appium支持多种编程语言，使测试人员能够使用他们最熟悉的语言。 这种灵活性允许与现有测试框架和工具无缝集成。

  • 内置手势和触摸操作：Appium 提供了在移动设备上执行手势和触摸操作的内置方法，例如滑动、点击、捏合和滚动。 这允许在测试期间模拟真实的用户交互。

  • 真实设备和仿真器/模拟器：Appium 允许您在真实设备以及仿真器/模拟器上进行测试。 这为根据可用性、成本和特定测试要求选择测试环境提供了灵活性。

  • 与测试框架集成：Appium 与 TestNG 和 JUnit 等流行测试框架完美集成，支持高级测试管理、并行测试执行和测试报告功能。

    • 使用 Appium 的优点：

    1. 开源和活跃的社区：Appium 是一个开源工具，拥有庞大且活跃的社区。 这确保了持续更新、错误修复和社区支持。

    2. 跨平台能力：Appium允许您编写一次测试并在多个平台上运行它们，减少了跨平台测试所需的工作量。

    3. 支持本机和Web应用程序：Appium支持本机和Web应用程序的测试，为测试不同类型的移动应用程序提供了灵活性。

    4. 广泛的语言支持：Appium 支持多种编程语言，允许测试人员利用他们现有的技能并使用他们选择的语言。

    使用 Appium 的缺点：

    1. 复杂的设置和配置：设置 Appium 和配置测试环境可能很复杂，尤其是在处理不同的移动平台、SDK 和设备配置时。

    2. 对 Windows 桌面应用程序的有限支持：

    虽然Appium支持在不同平台上测试移动应用程序，但它对测试Windows桌面应用程序的支持是有限的。

    1. 对 Appium 服务器的依赖：Appium 需要运行 Appium 服务器才能执行测试。 这引入了对服务器的依赖，并为测试设置增加了额外的复杂性。

      4. 测试执行跟踪：执行测试用例并在 TestRail 中跟踪其进度。 记录测试结果，包括通过、失败、阻止或重新测试。 捕获测试执行详细信息，例如测试运行持续时间、测试环境信息和任何相关注释。

    6. 崩溃报告：当您的应用程序在用户设备上崩溃时，TestFairy 会自动捕获崩溃报告。 它提供详细的崩溃日志、堆栈跟踪和设备信息，使您能够快速诊断和修复问题。 崩溃报告有助于确定崩溃的根本原因，并根据其影响确定错误修复的优先级。

    8. 用户反馈和错误报告：TestFairy 包含的功能使用户能够直接在应用程序内提供反馈、报告错误或提出改进建议。 用户可以添加评论、注释和屏幕截图，以提供有关遇到的问题的上下文信息。 这有助于收集用户反馈、确定错误修复的优先顺序并解决可用性问题。

    4. User Testing and Feedback : Testers can install the app on their devices and provide feedback directly through the TestFairy SDK integrated into the app. Testers can report bugs, add comments, and provide annotations or screenshots to provide comprehensive feedback.

    5. Session Analysis : Review session recordings to understand user interactions, identify usability issues, and reproduce reported bugs. Analyze session data to gain insights into user behavior and optimize app design and performance.

    6. Crash Analysis : Monitor crash reports and analyze crash logs to identify and prioritize critical issues. TestFairy provides detailed crash information, including device details and stack traces, to help debug and fix crashes.

    7. Performance Monitoring : Monitor key performance metrics provided by TestFairy, such as CPU usage, memory consumption, network requests, and battery usage. Identify performance bottlenecks and optimize app performance based on the collected data.

    Pros of using TestFairy:

    1. Simplified App Distribution : TestFairy simplifies the distribution of mobile app builds to testers, making it easy to share app versions for testing and gather feedback.

    2. Session Recordings : Session recordings provide visual context and help understand user interactions and identify usability issues or bugs.

    3. Crash Reporting : TestFairy's automatic crash reporting provides detailed crash logs, making it easier to diagnose and fix issues quickly.

    4. Performance Monitoring : The performance monitoring capabilities of TestFairy help identify performance bottlenecks and optimize app performance for a better user experience.

    5. User Feedback and Bug Reporting : TestFairy's feedback and bug reporting features allow users to provide feedback directly within the app, enabling efficient communication and collaboration between testers and developers.

    Cons of using TestFairy:

    1. Limited Automation Testing Capabilities : TestFairy does not provide built-in automation testing capabilities. It focuses more on app distribution, session recording, feedback collection, and monitoring.

    2. Dependency on User Participation : The effectiveness of TestFairy relies on user engagement and active participation in providing feedback and reporting bugs.

    8. Sauce Labs


    Sauce Labs is a cloud-based automation testing platform that provides a comprehensive solution for testing web and mobile applications. It offers a wide range of features and integrations to support automated testing in DevOps.

    Features of Sauce Labs:

    1. Cross-Browser and Cross-Platform Testing : Sauce Labs allows you to perform automated testing on a vast combination of browsers, operating systems, and devices. This ensures your application is tested across different environments to uncover any compatibility issues.

    2. Real Device Testing : With Sauce Labs, you can test your mobile applications on real devices rather than relying solely on emulators or simulators. This helps ensure accurate testing and provides a realistic user experience.

    3. Parallel Test Execution : Sauce Labs enables parallel execution of automated tests, allowing you to run multiple tests simultaneously. This significantly reduces the overall testing time and improves efficiency.

    4. Selenium and Appium Support : Sauce Labs provides seamless integration with popular automation frameworks like Selenium and Appium. This allows you to write and execute tests using your preferred framework and language.

    5. Video and Screenshots : Sauce Labs records video of test executions, capturing every step of the test. It also captures screenshots at different stages, providing visual evidence of test results. This helps in debugging and troubleshooting issues.

    6. CI/CD Integrations : Sauce Labs integrates with popular CI/CD tools like Jenkins, Bamboo, and CircleCI, allowing you to seamlessly incorporate automated tests into your continuous integration and delivery pipelines.

    7. Performance Testing : Sauce Labs offers performance testing capabilities that allow you to simulate high user loads and measure the performance of your application. This helps identify performance bottlenecks and ensures your application can handle heavy traffic.

    8. Debugging and Collaboration : Sauce Labs provides features like live debugging and session sharing, allowing testers and developers to collaborate effectively during test execution. This helps in identifying and resolving issues quickly.

    9. Analytics and Reporting : Sauce Labs generates detailed reports and analytics, providing insights into test results, test coverage, and overall test quality. These reports help track testing progress and make data-driven decisions.

    Applying and Using Sauce Labs in DevOps:
    To apply and use Sauce Labs in a DevOps environment, follow these steps:

    1. Account Setup : Sign up for a Sauce Labs account and set up your project. Configure your testing environment, including the browsers, platforms, and devices you want to test on.

    2. Automation Test Creation : Write your automated tests using the Selenium or Appium framework, using the programming language of your choice. Sauce Labs supports multiple programming languages, including Java, Python, JavaScript, and more.

    3. Test Execution : Configure your tests to execute on the Sauce Labs platform. You can either upload your test scripts or integrate Sauce Labs with your CI/CD pipeline to trigger tests automatically after each build.

    4. Test Monitoring : Monitor the progress of your tests on the Sauce Labs dashboard. You can view live test executions, access video recordings, and screenshots of each test to identify any issues or failures.

    5. Debugging and Issue Resolution : If a test fails, you can leverage Sauce Labs' debugging capabilities to identify the root cause. You can access the live test session, interact with the application, and analyze logs and other debugging tools.

    6. Integration with CI/CD : Integrate Sauce Labs with your CI/CD tools like Jenkins or Bamboo to trigger tests automatically after each build. This ensures continuous testing as part of your automated pipeline.

    7. Performance Testing : Utilize Sauce Labs' performance testing capabilities to simulate high user loads and measure the performance of your application. Analyze the performance results to identify bottlenecks and optimize your application's performance.

    Pros of using Sauce Labs:

    1. Broad Testing Coverage : Sauce Labs supports a wide range of browsers, platforms, and devices, allowing you to achieve comprehensive test coverage.

    2. Real Device Testing : The ability to test on real devices ensures accurate testing and provides a realistic user experience.

    3. Parallel Test Execution : The ability to run tests in parallel reduces testing time and improves overall efficiency.

    4. Integration with CI/CD : Sauce Labs seamlessly integrates with popular CI/CD tools, enabling continuous testing in your automated pipeline.

    5. Collaboration and Debugging : Sauce Labs provides features for live debugging and session sharing, facilitating effective collaboration between testers and developers.

    Cons of using Sauce Labs:

    1. Cost : Sauce Labs is a paid service, and the pricing may be a consideration for some organizations.

    2. Dependency on Internet Connection : Since Sauce Labs is a cloud-based solution, it relies on a stable internet connection for test execution. Any disruptions in the network may impact testing.

    9. LambdaTest


    LambdaTest is a cloud-based automation testing tool that offers a comprehensive platform for cross-browser and cross-platform testing of web applications. It provides a wide range of features and integrations to facilitate testing in DevOps.

    Features of LambdaTest:

    1. Cross-Browser Testing : LambdaTest allows you to test your web applications across a vast combination of browsers, versions, and operating systems. This ensures compatibility and consistency of your application's performance across different browsers.

    2. Real-time Testing : With LambdaTest, you can perform real-time testing by interacting with the application in real-time on the selected browser and operating system. This helps in identifying visual and functional issues during the testing process.

    3. Automated Testing : LambdaTest supports automated testing using popular frameworks like Selenium and Appium. You can write and execute automated test scripts using your preferred programming language and framework.

    4. Parallel Test Execution : LambdaTest enables parallel test execution, allowing you to run multiple tests simultaneously. This significantly reduces the overall testing time and improves efficiency.

    5. Visual Testing : LambdaTest offers visual testing capabilities, allowing you to compare screenshots of your web application across different browsers and versions. This helps in detecting any visual inconsistencies or layout issues.

    6. Responsive Testing : LambdaTest allows you to test the responsiveness of your web application by simulating different screen sizes and resolutions. This ensures that your application renders correctly on various devices.

    7. Integration and Collaboration : LambdaTest integrates with popular project management and collaboration tools such as Jira, Slack, and Trello. This enables seamless collaboration and communication between testers, developers, and other stakeholders.

    8. CI/CD Integrations : LambdaTest integrates with popular CI/CD tools like Jenkins, Travis CI, and CircleCI. This allows you to incorporate automated tests into your continuous integration and delivery pipelines.

    9. Network Simulation : LambdaTest allows you to simulate different network conditions such as 3G, 4G, and Wi-Fi to test the performance and functionality of your web application under different network environments.

    Applying and Using LambdaTest in DevOps:
    To apply and use LambdaTest in a DevOps environment, follow these steps:

    1. Account Setup : Sign up for a LambdaTest account and configure your testing preferences. Set up your desired browsers, operating systems, and testing environments.

    2. Test Script Creation : Write your test scripts using popular automation frameworks like Selenium or Appium, using the programming language of your choice.

    3. Test Execution : Configure your tests to execute on the LambdaTest platform. You can upload your test scripts or integrate LambdaTest into your CI/CD pipeline to trigger tests automatically after each build.
